title.rendered: ServiceTitan Alternatives (2026): 5 Options That Cost Less

content.rendered:

Field Service Software Alternatives

ServiceTitan Alternatives (2026): 5 Options That Cost Less

ServiceTitan costs more per month than most contractors spend on a technician’s salary. Here are five platforms worth evaluating — most don’t require a 36-month contract or a five-figure onboarding fee.

Research updated: Apr 2026Alternatives compared: 5✔ COMPARISON

Try Jobber Free for 14 Days →

ServiceTitan is purpose-built for large HVAC, plumbing, and electrical operations — and it is priced accordingly. Based on community-reported data, contractors pay $245–$500+ per technician per month, plus implementation fees of $5,000–$50,000+, on 12-to-36-month contracts. A 10-technician team with Marketing Pro and Phones Pro can spend $5,200+ per month — over $60,000 a year just on software. (For the full cost breakdown, see our ServiceTitan pricing guide.)

If you got a ServiceTitan quote and felt the floor drop, you are not alone. Most contractors under 20 technicians do not need — and cannot justify — that level of spend. Here are five alternatives that cover the core workflows most field service businesses need, at a fraction of the cost.

Why Contractors Look for ServiceTitan Alternatives

The search for alternatives is not about whether ServiceTitan is good software. It is. The issue is fit. Five patterns drive contractors away from ServiceTitan — or keep them from signing up in the first place:

  1. Price: $245–$500+ per tech per month (community-reported) plus $5,000–$50,000+ in implementation fees. For a 10-tech team, Year 1 can exceed $80,000 before add-ons. That is a significant investment for any operation under $2M in annual revenue.
  2. Contract lock-in: 12–36 month minimum commitments with early termination penalties equal to 100% of the remaining contract value. Getting out costs as much as staying in.
  3. Implementation complexity: Onboarding timelines of 6 weeks to 6+ months. Multiple BBB complainants report paying for months of subscription while never becoming fully operational.
  4. Over-featured for smaller teams: ServiceTitan itself states the platform is “not optimized for a company with 3 or fewer technicians.” Contractors under 15 techs consistently report using a fraction of what they pay for.
  5. Opaque pricing: You cannot see a number without sitting through a sales demo. For contractors who want to compare options before committing time, that is a real barrier.

The 5 Best ServiceTitan Alternatives

1. Jobber — Best Overall for Most Contractors Under 15 Techs

Why it is a genuine alternative: Jobber covers the core workflows most field service businesses run on — scheduling, dispatching, quoting, invoicing, client communication, and payments — with transparent pricing, a 14-day free trial, and no implementation fee. You can sign up and be dispatching jobs the same day.

Where ServiceTitan requires dedicated office staff to manage its complexity, Jobber is designed for owner-operators and small teams who need software that works without a project manager. The mobile app is consistently rated among the best in the category (4.5/5 on both iOS and Android app stores), and the client-facing experience — online booking, quote approval, automated reminders, payment collection — is polished enough to make a 3-person crew look like a much bigger operation.

Pricing vs. ServiceTitan:

What you give up vs. ServiceTitan: No marketing attribution (source-to-revenue tracking), no enterprise dispatch optimization, no AI-powered recommendations (Titan Intelligence), and no pricebook with automatic cost updates. Jobber’s reporting is solid but does not approach ServiceTitan’s depth for KPI dashboards and technician performance scoring.

Best for: Residential service businesses with 1–15 technicians across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, landscaping, cleaning, and general trades. Especially strong if you value fast setup and a clean mobile experience over enterprise-grade analytics.

Read our full Jobber review or see the head-to-head in ServiceTitan vs. Jobber.

Try Jobber Free for 14 Days →

2. Housecall Pro — Best for Home Service with Marketing Automation

Why it is a genuine alternative: Housecall Pro is the closest alternative to ServiceTitan for contractors who care about the marketing and customer acquisition side of their business. Features like automated review requests, email and postcard marketing, customer equipment tracking, and the visual pricebook are available at a fraction of ServiceTitan’s cost.

HCP’s biggest advantage over Jobber and Service Fusion for ServiceTitan shoppers is its marketing automation layer. If your ServiceTitan evaluation was partly driven by Marketing Pro’s campaign tracking and review generation, Housecall Pro’s Essentials plan ($149/mo) includes postcards, email marketing, and premium review management — not as deep as ServiceTitan’s marketing attribution, but genuinely functional at 95%+ less cost.

Pricing vs. ServiceTitan:

What you give up vs. ServiceTitan: HCP’s marketing tools are functional but do not provide ServiceTitan’s granular source-to-revenue attribution. Advanced dispatch optimization for large fleets is not available. The reporting layer is basic compared to ServiceTitan’s KPI dashboards. And the per-user pricing can add up once you exceed the included user count on each tier — MAX charges $35/month per additional user.

Best for: Home service businesses with 1–10 technicians that prioritize marketing automation, review generation, and client-facing professionalism. Strongest in HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and cleaning. Particularly good if you were attracted to ServiceTitan partly for its marketing capabilities but cannot justify the Marketing Pro price tag.

Read our full Housecall Pro review or see the head-to-head in Housecall Pro vs. ServiceTitan.

Try Housecall Pro Free for 14 Days →

3. Service Fusion — Best for 10–50 Tech Operations Wanting Flat-Rate Pricing

Why it is a genuine alternative: Service Fusion is the only platform on this list that offers unlimited users on every plan. That matters most for growing operations. If you have 15 technicians, 3 dispatchers, and 2 office managers, Service Fusion charges the same $208/month (Starter, annual) whether you add 5 users or 50. ServiceTitan would charge per tech, and Jobber and HCP both cap users by tier.

For contractors in the 10-to-50-tech range who are evaluating ServiceTitan specifically because they have outgrown per-user pricing on smaller platforms, Service Fusion is the most direct alternative. The platform covers scheduling, dispatching, estimates, invoicing, job costing (Plus tier), QuickBooks integration, and project management — the core operational workflows most field service businesses need.

Pricing vs. ServiceTitan:

What you give up vs. ServiceTitan: No AI-powered dispatch or recommendations. No Marketing Pro-level marketing attribution. Reporting is functional but not enterprise-grade. GPS fleet tracking is a separate add-on on all tiers. The mobile app is not as polished as Jobber’s or HCP’s. No free trial means you have to commit to at least one month to evaluate.

Best for: Mid-size field service operations (10–50 technicians) where predictable flat-rate pricing matters more than enterprise analytics. Especially strong for HVAC, plumbing, and electrical shops that are growing fast and do not want per-user fees eating their margin as they hire.

For more detail, see our best contractor software roundup.

See Service Fusion Pricing →

4. FieldEdge — Best for HVAC-Specific Operations

Why it is a genuine alternative: FieldEdge is the trade-specific option. While Jobber, HCP, and Service Fusion serve multiple home service verticals, FieldEdge was built specifically for HVAC, plumbing, and electrical contractors. Its strength is in the workflows that matter most to those trades: flat-rate pricebook management, service agreement tracking, equipment history, and QuickBooks integration that has been refined over decades of use in those specific verticals.

If your ServiceTitan evaluation was driven by needing trade-specific depth — particularly around service agreements and equipment tracking — FieldEdge delivers those capabilities at a lower per-user cost without the enterprise overhead.

Pricing vs. ServiceTitan:

What you give up vs. ServiceTitan: No Titan Intelligence or AI-powered recommendations. No Marketing Pro-equivalent marketing attribution. The dispatch board is functional but not as advanced as ServiceTitan’s automated dispatch optimization for large fleets. FieldEdge relies on third-party tools for GPS fleet tracking and review management, which adds cost. The platform is also narrower — it serves HVAC, plumbing, and electrical well, but does not extend to other home service verticals.

Best for: HVAC, plumbing, and electrical contractors with 5–20 technicians who want trade-specific pricebook and service agreement tools without the enterprise price tag. Particularly strong for shops that sell and manage service agreements as a core part of their revenue model.

Note: CSH does not have an affiliate relationship with FieldEdge. For a deeper look, see our FieldEdge review and FieldEdge vs. ServiceTitan.

5. Workiz — Best for Teams Needing Inventory and Dispatch Workflow

Why it is a genuine alternative: Workiz occupies a middle ground between Jobber’s simplicity and ServiceTitan’s enterprise depth. Its strongest differentiators are inventory management, equipment tracking, and a capable dispatch workflow — features that Jobber and HCP either lack or limit to higher tiers. The platform also offers AI-powered scheduling (Genius Scheduling) and an AI answering service on the Pro plan, which puts automated intelligence within reach without ServiceTitan’s price tag.

Workiz also offers a free Lite plan (2 users, limited to 20 jobs/month) that lets you test the platform before committing — something neither ServiceTitan nor Service Fusion offers.

Pricing vs. ServiceTitan:

What you give up vs. ServiceTitan: Workiz’s user caps are low — the Standard plan includes only 5 users, and extra users cost $46–$54/month each. That per-user cost can add up fast for larger teams. The marketing tools are basic compared to both ServiceTitan and Housecall Pro. Reporting is not as deep as ServiceTitan’s enterprise analytics. And the platform is not as widely adopted, which means fewer peer references and community resources.

Best for: Field service teams with 3–10 technicians that need inventory management, equipment tracking, and AI-assisted scheduling without the enterprise overhead. Strongest for locksmith, garage door, appliance repair, and similar service verticals where parts inventory and dispatch workflow matter more than marketing attribution.

Note: CSH does not currently have an affiliate relationship with Workiz.

Side-by-Side Comparison Table

* ServiceTitan and FieldEdge pricing is not published publicly. Figures are community-reported estimates.

What You Genuinely Give Up vs. ServiceTitan

It would be dishonest to pretend these alternatives match ServiceTitan feature-for-feature at the top end. They do not. ServiceTitan has capabilities that no platform on this list replicates:

Titan Intelligence. AI-powered business recommendations, revenue forecasting, and performance scoring based on your actual operational data. None of the alternatives offer anything this sophisticated. Jobber’s AI Receptionist and Workiz’s Genius Scheduling handle specific tasks, but they do not approach Titan Intelligence’s breadth.

Marketing attribution depth. Marketing Pro tracks ad spend from impression to booked job to closed revenue — by channel, by campaign, by keyword. It can tell you exactly which Google Ads campaign generated $47,000 in booked revenue last quarter. Housecall Pro’s marketing tools send postcards and collect reviews. They do not provide source-to-revenue attribution at ServiceTitan’s level.

Enterprise dispatch optimization. For a 30-truck fleet with complex scheduling constraints, ServiceTitan’s Dispatch Pro offers automated optimization that the alternatives cannot match. Jobber, HCP, and Service Fusion all have dispatch boards, but they are primarily manual or semi-automated.

Enterprise-grade CRM and pipeline management. ServiceTitan’s Sales Pro provides full pipeline management, proposal building, and sales performance tracking designed for home service sales teams. The alternatives have basic CRM functionality, but not this depth.

Advanced pricebook with automatic updates. Pricebook Pro provides pre-built, automatically-updated flat-rate pricing with visual good-better-best presentations. FieldEdge has comparable pricebook functionality for HVAC. The others offer basic pricebook tools but not automatic cost updates or AI-powered upsell recommendations.

The honest question is whether your operation actually uses — and generates ROI from — these specific capabilities. For most contractors under 20 technicians, the answer is no. The core workflows that drive daily revenue — scheduling, dispatching, quoting, invoicing, collecting payments, communicating with customers — are well covered by all five alternatives at 80–95% lower total cost.

When ServiceTitan Still Wins

Stay with ServiceTitan (or go ahead and sign up) if:

  • You run 20+ technicians with dedicated dispatchers, CSRs, and office managers
  • Annual revenue exceeds $2M and the business can absorb $50,000–$80,000+ in Year 1 software costs
  • You run significant advertising and need Marketing Pro’s source-to-revenue attribution to prove ROI
  • You have dedicated IT or admin staff to manage implementation and ongoing configuration
  • You need enterprise-grade reporting for investor, franchise, or multi-location oversight

At that scale, ServiceTitan’s depth is genuinely unmatched. The marketing attribution alone can pay for the platform if you are spending $10,000+ per month on advertising and need to know which channels generate revenue. The dispatch optimization makes a measurable difference when you are routing 20+ trucks across a metro area. And the reporting depth matters when you are managing technician performance, revenue forecasts, and operational KPIs across a large team.

The mistake is signing up for ServiceTitan at 8 technicians because you expect to grow into it. You will pay enterprise prices while operating at small-business scale, and the complexity will slow your team down rather than accelerate it. Start with a platform that fits today, and upgrade when the business demands it.

For the complete cost analysis, see our ServiceTitan pricing breakdown and the full ServiceTitan review.

Bottom Line

ServiceTitan is the most powerful field service platform available. It is also the most expensive, the most complex, and the least accessible for the majority of contractors. If you run a large operation with the revenue, staff, and marketing budget to justify it, ServiceTitan belongs on your short list.

For everyone else — and that is most contractors reading this — one of these five alternatives will cover 90%+ of what you actually need at 80–95% lower cost:

  • Jobber if you want the best overall platform for a team of 1–15 with fast setup and a strong mobile app.
  • Housecall Pro if marketing automation and review generation were part of what attracted you to ServiceTitan.
  • Service Fusion if you have 10–50 technicians and want flat-rate pricing with unlimited users.
  • FieldEdge if you run an HVAC, plumbing, or electrical shop and need deep trade-specific pricebook and service agreement tools.
  • Workiz if you need inventory management and AI-assisted scheduling for a smaller team.

Jobber and Housecall Pro both offer 14-day free trials. Service Fusion offers a demo with month-to-month commitment (no long-term contract). Start there, build a real workflow, and evaluate the platform with your actual jobs and team — not from a sales presentation.

Try Jobber Free for 14 Days →

FAQ

What is the best alternative to ServiceTitan?

For most contractors, Jobber is the best overall alternative. It covers scheduling, dispatching, quoting, invoicing, and client communication at $29–$299/month with no implementation fee and a 14-day free trial. For larger teams (10–50 techs) wanting unlimited-user flat-rate pricing, Service Fusion at $208/month is the strongest option. For teams that prioritize marketing automation, Housecall Pro fills that gap at a fraction of ServiceTitan’s Marketing Pro cost.

How much cheaper are ServiceTitan alternatives?

Significantly. A 10-technician team on ServiceTitan Essentials with Marketing Pro and Phones Pro pays approximately $5,200/month ($62,400/year) plus a $20,000 implementation fee. The same team on Jobber Grow Teams pays $299/month ($3,588/year) with no implementation fee. On Service Fusion Plus, it is $325/month ($3,900/year) with a $399 one-time setup. That is 80–95% less in total cost.

Can Jobber or Housecall Pro replace ServiceTitan?

For the core field service workflows — scheduling, dispatching, quoting, invoicing, payments, customer communication — yes. Both platforms cover these well. What they cannot replace is ServiceTitan’s enterprise-grade capabilities: Titan Intelligence (AI-powered business recommendations), Marketing Pro (source-to-revenue marketing attribution), advanced dispatch optimization for large fleets, and enterprise CRM/pipeline management. If your operation relies on those specific features, the alternatives are not a direct replacement.

Is Service Fusion good for large teams?

Service Fusion is the best option on this list for teams of 10–50+ technicians because of its unlimited-user pricing model. Whether you have 5 users or 50, the monthly price stays the same ($208–$533/month depending on tier). The trade-off is that the platform is not as polished as Jobber or HCP for smaller teams, GPS tracking is a separate add-on, and there is no free trial. It works best for mid-size operations that need predictable costs as they scale.

Does FieldEdge compete with ServiceTitan?

For HVAC, plumbing, and electrical contractors specifically, yes. FieldEdge offers trade-specific pricebook management, service agreement tracking, and equipment history at $100–$125/user/month — roughly half of ServiceTitan’s per-tech pricing. The gap is in enterprise features: FieldEdge does not have Titan Intelligence, Marketing Pro, or advanced fleet dispatch optimization. For a 5-to-20-tech trade-specific shop, FieldEdge covers the operational workflows well at lower cost. For larger or multi-trade operations, ServiceTitan’s breadth pulls ahead.

Should I choose Workiz over ServiceTitan?

If your team is under 10 technicians and you need inventory management, equipment tracking, and AI-assisted scheduling, Workiz at $225–$325/month is worth evaluating. The free Lite plan lets you test the platform with no commitment. The catch is that per-user fees ($46–$54/month each) add up quickly once you exceed the included user count. For teams over 10 techs, Service Fusion’s unlimited-user pricing or ServiceTitan’s enterprise depth are likely better fits.
